overview: NIDHI Prayas is a sub-scheme under the NIDHI (National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations) programme by the Department of Science and Technology (DST). It provides financial support in the form of grants to innovators for converting their ideas into prototypes. The scheme aims to promote innovation and entrepreneurship by supporting early-stage prototyping and proof-of-concept development.
objectives: To enable translation of innovative ideas to prototypes
To provide a platform for faster experimentation and modify approaches in the journey to market
To generate innovative solutions relevant to local and global problems
To attract youngsters to demonstrate their problem solving skills and abilities
To enhance the pipeline in terms of quality and quantity of innovative startups
eligibility: Individual innovators, startups, or entrepreneurs with a proof-of-concept or idea that requires prototyping support. The applicant must be an Indian citizen and the innovation should have potential for commercialization or social impact.
benefits: Financial support for prototyping, access to fabrication labs, mentorship, technical guidance, and networking opportunities with experts and investors.
financial_support: Grant-in-aid support up to INR 10 lakhs per innovator for prototyping and proof-of-concept development. The grant is disbursed in milestones based on progress review.
application_process: 1. Apply online through the NIDHI Prayas portal. 2. Submit idea/innovation details along with prototype development plan. 3. Undergo screening and evaluation by experts. 4. Shortlisted applicants present their idea before a selection committee. 5. Selected innovators receive grant approval and sign agreement. 6. Develop prototype with periodic reviews. 7. Submit final prototype and report for closure.
required_documents: Any prior work or IP details (if applicable)
caveats: Grant is only for prototyping and not for scaling or commercialization
Intellectual property generated remains with the innovator/institution
Funding is contingent on satisfactory progress reviews
Cannot be combined with other similar government grants for the same purpose
